subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the theme of nature’s power
Analyze the poet’s tone and imagery
Identify poetic devices like personification and alliteration
Interpret the message about human resilience
Relate the poem to real-life lessons of preparation and strength

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to the Poem and Poet
teaching_strategy: Brief discussion on storms and winds
Initial interpretation of title and theme
student_activities: Discuss experiences of strong wind or storms

lesson_plan.title: Analysis of Stanzas 1 and 2
topics_covered: Tone of warning and respect towards nature
student_activities: Summarize each stanza in own words

lesson_plan.title: Analysis of Stanzas 3 and 4
topics_covered: Imagery of building strong homes and bodies
Moral lesson of coexistence with nature
teaching_strategy: Discussion of moral and life lesson
Identify symbolic meaning of wind and human actions
student_activities: Write examples of human resilience from the poem
Discuss in pairs how people can face challenges

lesson_plan.title: Poetic Devices and Style
student_activities: Explain their effect on meaning and mood

lesson_plan.title: Revision, Reflection, and Recap
student_activities: Write personal reflection connecting poem to life
Answer short questions on theme and devices

teaching_aids: Charts showing poetic devices and examples
teacher_tip: Encourage students to visualize the wind’s power and relate it to human strength and preparedness to enhance understanding.
